title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Governor Maura Healey announced Massachusetts' public safety plan for the 2026 FIFA World Cup at a press conference.
The state has allocated $76 million in federal funds to address cybersecurity, emergency preparedness, and public health.
Gillette Stadium will host seven World Cup matches, with special transportation and security measures planned.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The New England Revolution supports White Stadium renovation, likening it to its proposed soccer-specific stadium in Everett.
The project, endorsed by Mayor Michelle Wu, involves a $135 million taxpayer investment and a $190 million contribution from Boston Legacy FC.
The Franklin Park Defenders filed a lawsuit, arguing the renovation violates Article 97, which protects public parkland in Massachusetts.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford man is in critical condition after being hit on I-95 in Veazie, Maine.
The man was struck while aiding an 18-year-old woman from Bangor whose car crashed due to snow.
Police say both the stranded driver and the Good Samaritan were hit by another vehicle in snowy conditions.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Rohane, 60, was arrested after a suspicious fire at his Monterey home early Wednesday morning.
The Monterey Fire Department responded to the fire at 340 Main Road around 3:50 a.m.; no injuries reported.
Fire and State Police officials suspect the fire was intentionally set; Rohane was found and taken into custody.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An 11-year-old yellow Labrador named Tessa was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth, Cape Cod.
Rescuers used pepperoni to coax Tessa out of the sinkhole after she was trapped for over two hours, officials said.
A suspected underground pipe leak caused the sinkhole which was 2 feet wide, preventing safe entry for rescuers.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue is seeking donations to support 16 neglected axolotls recovered from an overcrowding situation.
The axolotls, all named after cheeses, include Monterey and Ricotta, and are recovering from missing limbs and infections.
Formed in 2024, the nonprofit focuses on axolotl rehabilitation and adoption through a New England network of foster parents.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein discusses personal stories of imperfections in relationships in her Love Letters newsletter.
A24 is hosting a free screening of "The Drama" with Zendaya and Robert Pattinson in Somerville.
Yowei Shaw shares a story about a dining table burn mark symbolizing growth in her marriage.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opened a new student-run cosmetology salon with 25 stations for hands-on training.
The salon allows students to gain real-world experience working with public clients under instructor supervision.
Students can earn 1,000 training hours toward state licensure and a professional cosmetology career.

title: CeCarré brings a $48 gold-and-caviar-topped pizza to the Pru
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: CeCarré Pizza & Provisions opened at the Prudential Center, featuring a $48 gold-and-caviar-topped pizza called La Zarina.
The pizzeria offers Roman-style, crispy oval-shaped pizzas with unique toppings like kimchi, Gochujang, and hot dogs.
CeCarré is a rebranding effort by owner Nina Patel, replacing the short-lived Descendant Pizza location at the Prudential Center.

title: Mass. woman killed in 6-vehicle crash in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Massachusetts woman, 69, died in a six-vehicle crash on Interstate 75 in Ochopee, Florida.
The collision was triggered by a semi-truck unable to stop in time, impacting multiple vehicles.
Two men, 71 and 57, were seriously injured, and the crash is currently under investigation.
